target.name: Anduril Industries

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 18 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Anduril Industries is a Series H defense and security company currently valued at $61B, having raised over $11B to date. The target operates in a sector with 18 identified peers, though the company's scale and stage present a constrained comparable universe. Public comps number two (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS), while eight private peers have been identified, including Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, Quantum-Systems, and Flock Safety.

Peer-median valuation methodology proved inapplicable in this instance. Despite identifying 18 same-sector peers, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables yielded fewer than the three datapoints required for statistical reliability. The target's Series H stage and current $61B valuation isolate it from most private peers in the defense sector, which operate at earlier stages or materially different enterprise values. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise without meaningful analytical insight.

The constrained peer set reflects a structural challenge: Anduril's capital deployment and valuation tier place it outside the narrow band of directly comparable private companies. A robust valuation assessment would require either (1) expansion into adjacent geographies or subsectors within defense and security, (2) heavier weighting of the two available public comparables with appropriate stage discounts, or (3) reliance on transaction multiples and revenue-based methods if financial data becomes available. Current data constraints prevent a triangulated valuation range with confidence.
